title: Double-needle Coptic binding
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — variations on unsupported-link sewing
notes: Two-needle variant of the Coptic stitch. Both needles start at the same signature head, work in opposite directions around the spine loop, and meet at the tail. Creates a tighter, more decorative chain. Used when decorative thread colour contrast is part of the design. Prerequisite: knowledge of the basic single-needle Coptic.
